content.problemStatement:
Lav has an array AAA of size NNN. He noticed that Chef is initially standing at the **first** index of the array.

While standing at the ithi^{th}ith index (1≤i<N)(1 \leq i \lt N)(1≤i<N) of the array, Chef can perform the following types of jumps:


Jump 1\texttt{Jump 1}Jump 1: Jump to the immediate next index jjj such that AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ have the **same** parity.
Jump 2\texttt{Jump 2}Jump 2: Jump to the immediate next index jjj such that AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ have **different** parity.
Given that Chef can perform Jump 2\texttt{Jump 2}Jump 2 **at most once**, Lav wants to find the **minimum** number of jumps required by the Chef to reach the **last** index of the array.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_{1},A_2, \ldots, A_{N}A1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of jumps required by the Chef to reach the **last** index of the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111:  The minimum number of jumps required by the Chef to reach the last index is 222.
Chef is initially standing at index 111.

Chef chooses Jump 2\texttt{Jump 2}Jump 2 and jumps to index 222 as it is the immediate next element with different parity.
Chef chooses Jump 1\texttt{Jump 1}Jump 1 and jumps to index 444 as it is the immediate next element with the same parity.

Test Case 222:  The minimum number of jumps required by the Chef to reach the last index is 111.
Chef is currently standing at index 111.

Chef chooses Jump 1\texttt{Jump 1}Jump 1 and jumps to index 444 as it is the immediate next element with the same parity.
